item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An SCP that allows any key to work on any lock, even if they don't match? Seems like it'd make short work of SCP-📦-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for necroposting, but this might be interesting.
If SCP-055 does not allow anybody to remember what it is… Would not SCP-2660 provide the ultimate knowledge of what SCP-055 is not?
All the information about what SCP-055 is not will still be truth, thus not going against effect of SCP-2660.
SCP-2660 will with its effect provide the only thing that can be remembered about SCP-055-What SCP-055 is not, in its fullest.

TL:DR-Your brain will explode after all the information about what SCP-055 is not.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This one has a largely self-contained story, so I'm going to limit my suggestions to in-line links that are going to be rather non-invasive. Two spots come to mind. First, the mentions of CotBG can be linked to their Hub. Second, the mention of MTF Nu-7 can be linked to their official spot on the MTF list. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2660 was recovered following a Foundation raid on a known headquarters of the Maxwellist sect of the Church of the Broken God1. While the raid was otherwise successful, it was suspected by memetic analysts that MTF Nu-7 "Hammer Down" may have been exposed to cognitohazardous material during the operation. Cautionary amnestic treatments were utilized, but subsequent treatment evaluations revealed that some memories remained, all dealing with a lamp briefly spotted in the center of a "worship" hall. The object was secured and designated SCP-2660.

To this:

SCP-2660 was recovered following a Foundation raid on a known headquarters of the Maxwellist sect of the Church of the Broken God2. While the raid was otherwise successful, it was suspected by memetic analysts that MTF Nu-7 "Hammer Down" may have been exposed to cognitohazardous material during the operation. Cautionary amnestic treatments were utilized, but subsequent treatment evaluations revealed that some memories remained, all dealing with a lamp briefly spotted in the center of a "worship" hall. The object was secured and designated SCP-2660.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. While many orders of the Church of the Broken God believe in a deity with a fractured physical body, the Maxwellist sect worships a deity composed of information and believes that this deity manifests through human connectivity. As such, the internet and other global communication networks are of particular interest to Maxwellist worshippers.
2. While many orders of the Church of the Broken God believe in a deity with a fractured physical body, the Maxwellist sect worships a deity composed of information and believes that this deity manifests through human connectivity. As such, the internet and other global communication networks are of particular interest to Maxwellist worshippers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had an idea for an SCP once that was going to be an indivisible concept- an object whose likeness refused to be partially captured. If you were brought within line of sight of only a tiny part of its surface, you would nevertheless see and remember the whole thing; if you took a photograph of part of it, you would find that the image contained the full likeness, even if it extended beyond the edge of the storage media.

This does a lot more with the concept than I had ever thought of. Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thanks for clarifying. +1 from me then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They know there shouldn't be an image. But they can't get rid of it. Can't hide the article either, it'll just come up again. For all we know, the article itself is becoming more detailed by the minute thanks to the self-clarifying effect, and we can't change it. That's why they stopped testing, because they realized that everything they cataloged was just making it all worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article, but I feel that it is problematic in that the article itself would constitute an infohazard. I understand it would be difficult to write an article without giving a clear description but why would someone write all this information down in the main database if information about the object is essentially how it spreads? Why is there a photograph when it's clear that photographs of 2660 are dangerous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback!

And yeah, I created the SERS when I couldn't find any existing device that did the same thing. Feel free to use it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wishing it was something more interesting than a lamp, given how cool the effects are, but you made the lamp fit into the lore enough to justify it being there. I think the only thing I'd criticize is the doctor's dialogue in the interview, but that's just a feeling more than anything I can pin down. Well done!

By the way, did you create the SERS, and if so, do you mind me using one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started reading, expecting an 'I love lamp' gag. Was pleasantly surprised it was far more complex and terrifying. Happily +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This actually dovetails nicely with the sometimes-seen headcanon of SCP-426 being the Broken God's self-awareness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy to help! I like the way this turned out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that the description should mention what happens to destruction of descriptions of the SCP, like burning a paper which has a written description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting with reservations despite the continued proliferation of SRA derived technology, on this article's own considerable merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Something that can only be remembered as something that cannot be remembered in any other way."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That idea was suggested, but it never made it into my draft. Once upon a time I thought of putting this in a collaborative extended experiment log, but decided that with the 2660-1 effect being rather finicky and with the end of the article implying that testing is going to be super restricted, it was for the best not to go there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most likely, your supervisor would slap you for trying to crosstest with a Keter-class object.

But seriously, I don't know. I'd reckon that the two opposite effects would wreak havoc on your mind (you cannot forget it but you cannot remember it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if you put this thing in the same room with 055?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the original idea, like it now. I do think it's a shame that you cut out the "Drs X, Y, and Z are exposed." "I see two people and Dr. X."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the draft on the forums, and was a little ambivalent (mostly around the exact nature of the effect, the new technobabble devices which seemed a little unnecessary, and not understanding the "Maxwellist" references). But on reading it again, I'm not as worried by any of that - the concept is strong and well realised, and the writing is clear enough that you don't need to over-sell the danger.

I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…they see only their own shadows, or the shadows of one another, which the fire throws on the opposite wall of the cave…

- Plato's Republic

Special thanks to EldritchCyanide, Decibelles, and all others who reviewed in chat and assisted during the final hours.

Also thanks to Baby_Boo, who gave me a good idea for a line, and I told them I'd credit them.

Art by SunnyParallax
Art by SunnyParallax

EDIT: 159 days after posting, I realized I have a redaction in there that shouldn't be redacted. It's gone now.

EDIT 2: A continuation of sorts can be found here. As well, there's now a 2660 entry in the 2305-A collaborative log.

EDIT 3: As the old image wasn't properly licensed, it has been replaced. Source
